query: CHP Turkey leadership crisis Özel new party June 2026 latest developments
answer: As of June 15, 2026, Özgür Özel has NOT left CHP and has NOT founded a new political party. On May 27, 2026, he explicitly ruled out creating a new party, stating: 'We have no intention of founding a new party. We will stand by Atatürk's party and fight.' He urged CHP members not to resign or leave the party. The crisis stems from a May 21, 2026 court ruling (the 'absolute nullity' or 'mutlak butlan' decision) by the Ankara Regional Court of Appeal that invalidated the CHP's 38th Ordinary Congress (November 2023) and reinstated Kemal Kılıçdaroğlu as chairman, removing Özel. The situation as of mid-June 2026: (1) Özel retains the support of ~111 of 138 CHP MPs and was elected CHP parliamentary group chairman; (2) On June 11, 28 Özel-loyalist Party Assembly members resigned to trigger an extraordinary congress within 45 days under party statute; (3) Kılıçdaroğlu launched a formal congress process on June 11; (4) The two factions are holding parallel meetings — Özel in parliament, Kılıçdaroğlu at CHP headquarters; (5) No new party has been announced or founded by Özel or his allies. The dispute is over control of CHP, not about creating a splinter party.

sources.title: Republican People's Party absolute nullity crisis - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: On 21 May 2026, the Regional Court of Appeal in Ankara invalidated the 38th Ordinary and 21st Extraordinary Conventions of the CHP. The ruling removed CHP leader Özgür Özel and reinstated former leader Kemal Kılıçdaroğlu as chairman.

sources.title: Özgür Özel - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: Following a court ruling in May 2026, his leadership of the CHP was subject to absolute nullification, triggering the 2026 Turkish absolute nullity crisis. Özel was reelected as leader of the CHP on 21 September 2025 at a special congress.

sources.title: Suspended CHP chairman Ozgur Ozel rules out founding a new party
sources.snippet: Ozel said on May 27, 2026 in Manisa: 'We have no intention of founding a new party. We will stand by Atatürk's party and fight.' He called on all party members not to resign.

sources.title: Suspended Turkish opposition leader's team resigns from party assembly
sources.snippet: On June 11, 2026, 28 members of CHP's 57-seat Party Assembly loyal to Özel resigned, reducing membership below the two-thirds quorum required under party statute, triggering a legal obligation for an extraordinary congress within 45 days.

sources.title: Rival CHP factions convene separately amid growing political turmoil
sources.snippet: On June 9, 2026, CHP held two separate group meetings: Özel addressed lawmakers at Parliament while Kılıçdaroğlu spoke at CHP headquarters. 111 of 138 CHP deputies support Özel.

sources.title: Kilicdaroglu sets June 11 launch for main opposition CHP's new congress process
sources.snippet: Reinstated CHP Chairman Kılıçdaroğlu announced launching a new congress process starting with the Party Assembly meeting on June 11, 2026.

sources.title: Most Turks oppose court reinstating Kilicdaroglu as CHP leader: Poll
sources.snippet: MetroPOLL survey: 62.1% of respondents oppose the court annulment of CHP congress and Kılıçdaroğlu reinstatement; 74.5% of CHP voters opposed it. Even 54.4% of AKP supporters opposed the move.

sources.title: Inside CHP's 'plan B': A verdict that can rock Türkiye's political landscape
sources.snippet: April 1, 2026 article describing the pending court case and CHP's Plan B strategy. CHP Deputy Group Chairman Gökhan Günaydın stated 'our Plan B is ready' if an absolute nullity ruling is issued.

sources.title: What's happening in Türkiye's main opposition CHP: Leadership crisis, defections and drama
sources.snippet: Comprehensive September 2025 article tracing the CHP internal crisis from the 2023 congress through corruption allegations, trustee appointments, and leadership clashes.

key_facts: On May 21, 2026, the Ankara Regional Court of Appeal invalidated CHP's 38th Ordinary Congress (Nov 2023) and 21st Extraordinary Congress under 'absolute nullity' (mutlak butlan) doctrine
The court ruling removed Özgür Özel as CHP chairman and reinstated Kemal Kılıçdaroğlu
On May 24, 2026, Turkish police entered CHP headquarters with tear gas and rubber bullets to evict Özel's administration
On May 27, 2026, Özel explicitly ruled out founding a new party, saying 'We have no intention of founding a new party. We will stand by Atatürk's party and fight'
Özel retains overwhelming parliamentary support: ~111 of 138 CHP deputies back him; he was elected CHP parliamentary group chairman
On June 9, 2026, rival CHP factions held parallel meetings — Özel at Parliament, Kılıçdaroğlu at party headquarters
On June 11, 2026, 28 Özel-loyalist Party Assembly members resigned to trigger mandatory extraordinary congress within 45 days
Kılıçdaroğlu launched his own congress process on June 11, 2026
The Kılıçdaroğlu camp referred 9 Özel-aligned members to the High Disciplinary Board for expulsion on June 10, 2026
MetroPOLL survey: 62.1% of Turks oppose the court reinstatement of Kılıçdaroğlu; 74.5% of CHP voters oppose it
The ruling triggered a 6% stock market drop and $6-8 billion in state bank interventions to defend the lira
International condemnation: EU, Socialist International, PES, Germany, and Israel criticized the ruling as undermining democracy
Analysts say the crisis could trigger snap elections as early as autumn 2026
The crisis is widely viewed as part of a broader AKP strategy to weaken the opposition through the judiciary, following the arrest of Istanbul mayor Ekrem İmamoğlu in March 2025
No new party has been announced or founded by Özel or his allies as of June 15, 2026 — the battle is for control of CHP itself

revision_history.note: Major update: replaced placeholder/incomplete data with verified live source information from Wikipedia and Turkiye Today. Confirmed Özel explicitly ruled out founding new party on May 27, 2026. Added full timeline of absolute nullity crisis, parliamentary support numbers, Party Assembly resignations, polling data, economic impact, and international reactions.
